summary: Backend/audit persistence blocker is cleared by backend-audit-persistence: final memo export now requires canonical persisted gate snapshot and gate authorization audit events before final work product export. Gate Engine frontend behavior remains complete and focused gate tests pass against the persisted evidence contract.
last_cycle_summary: Backend/audit persistence landed canonical gate_results_persisted, final_export_gate_authorized, and final_export_gate_blocked audit actions plus final memo export fail-closed checks.
contractsChanged: Final memo export now evaluates GateResult[] before approval request and final save
Final memo work product content includes gateResults and gateSummary
Final memo approval checkpoint requestedPayload includes gateResults and gateSummary
Remote matter page displays adapter-derived final memo GateResult[] before export
GatePersistenceProvenance maps displayed gates to persisted source record refs and unresolved source requirements
Final memo approval/final content includes gateProvenance
GateChecklist displays expandable source_record_refs and unresolved_source_requirements detail for each provenance-backed gate
Focused gate tests assert final export fails closed when critical gates fail after approval while draft export remains available
Backend/audit persistence blocker documented for first-class GateResult/AuditEvent storage
Backend/audit persistence implemented canonical aletheia_audit_events gate snapshot contract
Final memo export authorization now resolves to persisted gateSnapshotAuditEventId and gateAuthorizationAuditEventId

risks: Gate inputs are still derived in remoteMatterTransforms rather than through the canonical AgentOps adapter acceptance path.
GateResult/GatePersistenceProvenance are still supplied by the frontend gate engine, but final memo authorization now uses server-written persisted gate audit events.
Full Aletheia UI smoke was not rerun in this backend persistence lane.
next_actions: Use full UI smoke as a later broad regression check if UI surfaces change.
